entry.title: Docker Image SQL Anywhere 17
entry.content:
I am beginner with Docker Containers and looking for an Image for have a containerized instance of SQL Anyhwhere 17.

I found one in Docker Hub and tried it, it is running, but it has the version 16, where can I find the 17 version, and is it possible to make my own image?

Another point which I dont understand, how would the license problem solved? Or the image itself does not need license, but only the client who is connecting to it would need it?

 

Any comment is welcomed.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HC - How to download Payment advice XML data?
entry.content:
Hi Lucy,

When I filter the payment method with *CGI_XML_CT*, it does not return any data.

Regards,

Tri

entry.title: Re: Cross Company Sales Return Process
entry.content:
Hi Vivek,

can you please indicate what scope item you are using for the processing of the intercompany return?.. If you are using Intercompany Process for Customer Returns (2F4), this best practice does include a step for quality inspection at either the intercompany site or at the customer site. Either one of these options could help in your case.

However, SF4 requires separate activation and additional manual setup steps.

Please elaborate. Thanks,

-Carlos

entry.title: Re: Integrate Sharepoint online with Datasphere
entry.content: Thanks Koray! For step 3 Create a connection from Open Connector to Sharepoint (https://help.openconnectors.ext.hana.ondemand.com/home/sharepoint-api-provider-setup) What is the redirect URI look like?

entry.title: Re: Problems with SAP Lumira application (version 2.4.0.4...
entry.content:
Hi Daniel,

in a side note the PAM for Lumira 2.4 states "SAP BW/4HANA FPS1 is supported from SAP Lumira 2.4 SP00 Patch 7+ Onwards. I think there is no way around upgrading to Patch 7 (and as prerequisite if applicable also upgrading your BI Platform to 4.3 SP3 Patch 6 or higher).

 

Kind regards

Jonas

entry.title: Re: No service provider was defined for CoCd XXXX
entry.content:
Solution: go to configure value mapping ->     namespace: /EDOMX     value mapping: SERVICE_PROVIDER

This for México.

Regards

entry.title: Interface from ECP to FI via Azure File Share
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently using SFTP to send payroll posting files from ECP to an external FI system.

Is it possible to use AZURE file share instead? What are the pre-requisites please?

Thank you,

entry.title: Adapt UI in Customizing Tenant
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

 

When we want to extend fields in an app we normally have to adapt UI. 

This would mean that we have to see the screen of the app itself for us to specifically place this field, but this has to be done in Customizing Tenant.

Some data customizations are hard to do in Customizing System due to the lack of data and have to do a lot of configuration.

Is there a better way to adapt UI or it really has to be done this way?  

 

Kind regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Adapt UI in Customizing Tenant
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

It is not prohibited to Adapt UI in Test or Productive Systems, however, you need to be careful and don’t mix systems or clients when you make UI changes.

When you adapt the UI of an app, in this case it’s important that you make the UI changes only in the test environment and then transport or copy them to your productive environment.

For example: Adapt the UI in the test system only and transport the changes to the productive system or Adapt the UI in the test client only and copy the changes to the productive client.

 

Your productive system or client doesn't prevent you from making UI changes there, but it's important that you do not mix these scenarios for one app (that is, making changes in both the test and the productive environment).

This would lead to severe UI adaptation conflicts.

If you don't have or don't use a test system or client, please do everything in the productive environment.

 

Kind regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Error opening Guided Development in VSCode
entry.content: yes, looks the latest is not available yet, so you have to downgrade to 1.93

entry.title: Re: Error opening Guided Development in VSCode

entry.title: SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Erline, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about me: I am a recent IT Management graduate with extensive knowledge in both business and technology. I currently hold six industry certifications, including CSM, ITILv4, and Security+, and I work as an Operations Analyst at a medical device company, where I gain hands-on experience in procurement, quality assurance, and much more.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m eager to expand my knowledge in all areas of SAP.

I’m especially excited about obtaining this certification because it will allow me to continue doing what I do best—delivering a wealth of knowledge and solutions to our customers while opening up broad career opportunities for me.

I’m also open to connecting with others who share an interest in technology, supply chain, and related fields.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

Best regards,

Erline Mercier
Operations Analyst

entry.title: [BYD] How to extend the label digit length to be 8 digits or more?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support

We want to use the labels on our logistics processes together with the scanner device, we want to be able to do stock counts, inbound and outbound tasks using the scanner, but on the GTIN field on the product master, it does not accommodate less that 8 digit number, but the system only generate 4 digit code number for bar code labels. your assistance will be more appreciated to point us on the right direction or providing a solution to us

Thanks,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: [BYD] How to extend the label digit length to be 8 di...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

I believe below help portal would answer your question.

Configuration: Bar Code Type UCC/EAN 128 | SAP Help Portal

 

Best regards,

Gavan

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: Transport a project between clients in Migration Cookpit
entry.content:
We need a help, to solve a problem.
We need you tell us the steps, exactly, how to transport a project between clients in Migration Cookpit. 
We need transport the project the client DH0-650  to DH0-550 and DH0-110. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Transport a project between clients in Migration Cookpit
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thanks for contacting SAP. I have picked this case manually to speed up.

For "Migrate Data Using Staging Table" approach, you can transfer the migration project to another client using LTMOM as per Using a Project in a Different Client | SAP Help Portal.

->Using a Project in a Different Client

By default, you can only view and edit projects in the client where they were created. It is only possible to edit a project in one client in the system. If you want to view and edit a project in a different client, you need to use the SAP S/4HANA migration object modeler (transaction LTMOM) to change the settings for a project so that it can be used in that client. To do this, open the relevant project and choose GoTo  Use Project in Different Client.

Note that all mapping tasks are deleted. This means that you need to download the mapping tasks before you proceed. You can then upload the mapping tasks in the new client.

Example

You create a migration project in client 100. You want to edit the project in client 200. In client 200, you open the project in transaction LTMOM and choose GoTo  Use Project in Different Client. In the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit, you can now view and edit the project in client 200. It is no longer possible to edit the project in client 100.

For "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System" approach, you cannot do this process using LTMOM. You need to create a new migration project manually in the new client.

Best regards,
Ron
SAP Support

entry.title: How to configure SAP Cloud Identity Services with Microsoft Entra for automatic user provisioning.
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have  a requirement to sync all users from SAP Cloud Identity Services to Microsoft Entra . 

Here are the steps which I followed 

1) Created new administrator as system and configured all the permissions.

 

2) Created a new  sourcing system  as type Local Identity Directory and  added Type field as Identity Provisioning under properties 

3) Created a new  Target System with all properties specified from following link( https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/target-microsoft-entra-id)

 

4) Configured real time identity provisioning 

5) Test connection is fine but when I click on provisioning .It shows 14 out of 19 users are provisioned but in provision logs it says entity skipped 

I am able to sync users from entra to SAP CIS but not from CIS to entra .Please let me know am I missing any configurations here ?

entry.title: Re: BW4HANA Open hub data for "JPY", "IDR" currency is no...
entry.content: Thank you Roland.

entry.title: About the Removal Tool for Maintenance Customers
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 


Thank you for your help. 
We have a customer who will no longer be covered by maintenance, and we have a question about how to handle this.
As a response, we are considering changing the users of customers who are no longer covered by maintenance to unusable,
but are there any tools or procedures on the SAP side for when a customer is no longer covered by maintenance?
Thank you for your confirmation.


----------


ご担当者様


お世話になっております。
保守対象外になるカスタマがあり、その対応について質問があります。
対応としては、保守対象外になったカスタマのユーザを使用不可に変更することを考えておりますが、
SAP側でカスタマが保守対象外となった際に対応するツールまたは手順はありますでしょうか。
ご確認よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: About the Removal Tool for Maintenance Customers
entry.content:
Dear *,

Yes, from SAP support side, you can change the users of customers who are no longer covered by maintenance to unusable, or remove or limit certain permissions for the users of customers no longer covered by maintenance.

For further contract-related questions or issues, Could you please contact SAP Order Management by e-mail for the contract-related issue? They do not work within the case application. You can check the KBA 1660069.
##1660069 - How to contact SAP Order Management for contract-related questions or issues

Best regards,
Leo
SAP Support

I am Sheena May Del Valle, from Pennsylvania, USA. I graduated with a degree in Business Administration in the Philippines. It is difficult to penetrate the corporate companies even with a degree because I have no corporate experience here in the US. Most of my background is running a small family business as well as my own business back in the Philippines.

I believe that with my experience, skills and education, I will succeed in the Tech Industry by dedicating my time in learning the SAP and continuously learning business trends. 

If given a chance, I would like to focus in SAP Supply Chain Management.

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello!

 

My name is Rafael, and I’m from Brazil. I am 20 years old and currently in my 4th semester majoring in Computer Science. Unfortunately, I lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for myself. However, I am determined to dig into SAP ABAP specifically. My interest was sparked by my own curiosity and the influence of my older brother, who has been a SAP ABAP developer for 5 years. 

 

I already have some practical experience with this programming language. I’ve completed several projects on my own and have been studying it for the last 10 months. During this period, I took courses from ABAP experts but never had the opportunity to learn from official SAP sources or obtain an SAP certification. I hope to achieve my first certification through this project.

 

Regards,

 

Rafael Ienne.

entry.title: Re: Everything you need to know about: SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub
entry.content:
@simcap  ,

 

1. is it possible to use standard library in SSF or can we upload the attributes manually?

Both! You could start with the free Skillsplex Library, that has around 10k skills. Its been around for a while and not recently updated, however this gives a good starting point and most skills are still relevant.

2. is it possible to implement intelligent hub without using JPB?(new implementation)

You could but not advisable, you won't get much value out of it. limited use case. 

3. in SSF, is there any standard library that we can find to enable TIH?

yes, Skillsplex. Its mentioned in my blog.

entry.title: Re: Extracting texts from GuiUserArea w/o ActiveX control (just built-in feature of iRPA)
entry.content: I've already encountered this type of issue, and I hope someone will answer it

entry.title: UNGA TAKES ON DIGITAL GOVERNANCE
entry.content:
UNGA TAKES ON DIGITAL GOVERNANCE

For New Yorkers, the end of September brings chaos every year -- traffic jams, road closures, VIP motorcades and no available tables at any decent restaurant on the east side. It’s not that easy to disrupt the operations of a city that hosts over 13 million people most workdays but it’s possible when world leaders gather at the UN Headquarters for the opening session of the UN General Assembly (UNGA). UNGA typically addresses a broad spectrum of global concerns -- war, poverty, climate change, global economy and sustainability. This year was no different. Conflicts in the Middle East, Ukraine and great power conflict dominated, but as has been true in recent years, digital policy issues muscled their way onto the crowded policy agenda. Two specific initiatives were advanced: the Global Digital Compact (GDC) and the UN Convention Against Cybercrime. This article will provide a basic overview of these programs and assess their potential impact on the digital regulatory landscape in 2025 and beyond.

Global Digital Compact

The centerpiece of UNGA 2024 was the Summit of the Future, a project to adapt global cooperation to the realities of the modern world. It took place on September 22 – 23 and resulted in the adoption of the Pact for the Future, a policy document which articulates a vision for international collaboration on themes including peace and security, sustainable development, climate change, digital cooperation, human rights, gender, youth and future generations, and the transformation of global governance. The Global Digital Compact, incorporated into the Pact as an addendum, is a comprehensive framework for governance of digital technology and artificial intelligence. It charts a collaborative roadmap for harnessing the potential of digital technology and reducing digital divides based on six (6) guiding principles: Universal Connectivity, Human Rights Online, Digital Inclusion and Equity, Safe and Secure Digital Environment, Digital Innovation for Sustainable Development, and Fostering Trust in Technology.

To achieve these principles, the GDC sets objectives and actions in each of the following five categories:

  • Expanding Digital Infrastructure Development, especially to underserved regions.
  • Establishing global regulatory frameworks that guide the use and ethical development of AI
  • Ensuring that data privacy rights are respected globally.
  • Combatting misinformation, hate speech, and cybercrime through regulations, transparency mandates, and real-time monitoring systems.
  • Promoting green technology initiatives and digital tools that combat climate change.

By adopting the Pact for the Future, to include the GDC, the UN is elevating data governance on the global diplomatic agenda. Nonetheless, both he Pact and the GDC are non-binding. As such, execution and enforcement of the principles contained in the GDC will only occur through national, regional and local legislation and regulation. Only time will tell whether member states will choose to take action on the objectives contained in the GDC and map their own regulatory vehicles to the GDC guidance.

UN Convention on Cybercrime:

On August 1, 2024, the UN Ad Hoc Committee on Cybercrime adopted the Comprehensive International Convention on Countering the Use of Information and Communications Technologies for Criminal Purposes (the “UN Convention Against Cybercrime”). This was the culmination of over five years of often contentious negotiation between two broad coalitions of nations with starkly different views of how such a treaty should be applied. Proposed by Russia in 2019, the Convention was initially opposed by signatories to the Budapest Convention on Cybercrime, the 2004 Council of Europe framework which established certain categories of cyber-crimes, established protocols of international cooperation and built safeguards to ensure civil liberties. Unable to prevent the UN convention from moving forward, Budapest signatories, such as the US, EU and others eventually joined the drafting process to ensure proper consideration of civil liberties and other provisions of concern.   

The current draft outlines multiple objectives focused around controlling the use of technology that can facilitate firearm and drug trafficking, terrorism, and other transnational crimes. It stresses a need for member state coordination on legislative fronts to enforce the provisions of the convention. Critics claim, however, that the text’s scope is too broad, allowing countries to apply it to offenses beyond what were typically considered cybercrimes in the past. Opponents include human rights and privacy advocates and big tech companies. Both factions have concerns over text that says authorities investigating crimes in any nation are entitled to electronic evidence and ISP data from other nations. Privacy groups have expressed concerns of inappropriate monitoring of global information flows. Industry leaders are also concerned that it could keep security researchers from reporting vulnerabilities they detect in networks and other tech systems because they will be more likely to fear prosecution under the treaty’s language.

Though no formal action was taken on the UN Convention during High-level Week in September, it was the subject of much discussion. Despite all the controversy, the convention is expected to be adopted by the UN General Assembly in late 2024 or early 2025. Once adopted, the treaty will be open for countries to sign and will enter into force 90 days after the 40th country ratifies it. Some countries may need parliamentary approval to ratify the treaty. Nonetheless, it will remain controversial and global application and enforcement of the treaty will likely remain uneven with different countries selectively interpreting provisions that suit politically expedient purposes.

entry.title: Re: Estimated Annual Usage
entry.content: how do we maintain the EAU and where?

entry.title: the value of group valuation for intercompany subcontracting is not correct
entry.content:
Hi, Guru,

We are encountering one issue regarding intercompany subcontracting and group valuation. We found that the output result of the group valuation is not correct for the intercompany subcontracting transactions. Here is the business scenario.
Company code A and company code B are manufacturing companies. Company C is a trading company. All these three companies are individual legal entities within one corporation group.
Company B issues subcontracting purchase orders to company A to produce finished products. The raw materials and packaging materials are provided from company B to company A as free of charge materials.
Company A produces the products with the raw materials and packaging materials that are provided by company B. So, the cost in the production orders is activity cost only (e.g., labor cost, depreciation cost, and other overhead cost, etc.)
Once the production orders are completed, the products are received in warehouse by goods receipt by company A. Subsequently company A issues goods to company B and issues billing documents to company B as the subcontracting service fee (manufacturing overhead cost, plus markup).
Company B posts good receipts against the subcontracting purchase orders and post invoice verification as well.
Company C issues intercompany STO to company B. Company B posts goods issue against the STO and issues the billing documents based the delivery notes from the STO. Consequently, the goods receipt and invoice verification are posted automatically in company C.
However, we found that even though the above scenario is the standard process that can be managed by the SAP system, the group valuation from company B and company C cannot reflect the activity cost components from company A. Rather than that, the cost components from company A displayed in company B or C is the subcontracting fee. That means the cost from the group valuation perspective is higher than the actual cost.
So, we are wondering if the group valuation can manage the scenario of intercompany subcontracting process? Is there a notes that we can implement to realize the group valuation for intercompany subcontracting business?

Looking forward your feedback

Liu Jun

entry.title: Re: Ho to create a Testuser for RAP App in BTP?
entry.content: you have use Maintain Employees app first, then  use maintain business users.

entry.title: Sales orders for semi-finished products cannot run out of planned orders after running MRP

I want to run the planned order through the sales order, but the display interface in MD04 is empty, but the planned order can be run through MD61. I checked that the master data MRP type is PD and policy is 70. Check that the plan category in the sales order is CP. I don't know what the problem is now. I hope you experts can help me look at this problem

entry.title: Re: How to mange and distribute fix in custom package to customers
entry.content: https://abapgit.org/ 😉

entry.title: Re: From Parallel Accounting to Universal Parallel Accounting and a focus on Parallel valuation
entry.content:
Very helpful material on the background for UPA (I love the std cost estimate for each acctg principle).  My concern has to do with the restrictions on using it, from the latest note indicating that Parallel COGM cannot be used, and EBRR must replace Results Analysis.

Since currency and valuation profiles are now obsolete, I consider this "1 ledger - 1 valuation" type of configuration.  Add as many as you need, and the ledger drives the valuation.

I would like more information on the best "no-regrets" setup for a greenfield implementation where the client is not 100% sure about going forward with UPA as it is a large step change but wants to keep the door open towards using it in the future.

And, can't wait for blog #2!

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE SDK Windows 11
entry.content:
Hi Jose,
Yes, SDK for SAP ASE 16.0 has certification on Windows 11 x64.
You can jump to Product Availability Matrix (PAM) page from KBA 2497029:
2497029 - How to check connectivity product certification information for SAP ASE SDK https://me.sap.com/notes/2497029

I attached the screenshot for PAM.

Hope this helps.
Best regards,
Masahiro

  • @odata.context: "$metadata#Customers",
  • value: [ ]

}

What could be the problem?

entry.title: Cannot connect from S/4HANA to CPI (NeO) via SOAP
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I cannot connect from S/4HANA to CPI (NeO) via SOAP even though I have a true S-ID account (error 401).

Could you please guide me in checking the token or something like that to fix this issue?

entry.title: Re: How to mange and distribute fix in custom package to...
entry.content:
I would point out to the customer that using Git for code management has got to be safer and more secure than relying on a developer to manually copy code from one place to the other.

I would also point out that using a tool to update ABAP artefacts in your development system is no different to having a developer do the same thing.

And finally, if using Git for code management is good enough for their non-ABAP development teams (which it almost certainly is) why isn't is good enough for the ABAP team?

😎

entry.title: Using BAPI delivery order to PGI through API
entry.content:
Hello Experts!!!

 Based on STO we create auto generated delivery order using API where BAPI ''BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CREATE_STO"  used. Right now similar way we need to complete the delivery order line wise using BAPI through API completed with PGI where update S.Loc (LGORT) and Picked Qty (PIKMG) .I created a custom BAPI and its work for 15 records after that its gave error.

Please guided me. Attachment given as reference.

entry.title: Re: How to get list all data in Tcode CKMLQS
entry.content: I try with ST05 and solved the problem. It's base on tables: ckmlhd mldoc ml4h_cds_mv003 ml4h_cds_mv004

entry.title: Re: Cross Company Sales Return Process
entry.content:
Hello Carlos, 

Thank You for your prompt response. 

For the intercompany return process, we are using the Intercompany Return Process for customer returns (2F4). Our business requirement is that whenever we do Post Goods Receipt for returns, material valuation should not happen immediately. It should only happen after quality inspection.

In the Customer Return Process (BKP), we have a suspend valuation option where we can control the material valuation.

So, my question is how we can achieve this requirement in the case of the Intercompany Return Process for customer returns (2F4).

Best Regards 

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Remote Client copy without Personal data (PA Tables)
entry.content: Hi Mari, Could you explain in details about the source production system client and target development system client details?

entry.title: Re: Company Certificate Management & Check Certificate Statuses in SAP
entry.content: I found this article highly informative and relevant to my work. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Query with Last Invoice Date
entry.content:
Hi,

try this

SELECT
	 OCRD.CardCode
	,OCRD.CardName
	,(
		SELECT
			MAX(T0.DocDate)
		FROM
			OPCH T0
		WHERE
			T0.CardCode = OCRD.CardCode
			AND
			T0.CANCELED = 'N'
	 ) AS [Last purchase invoice date]
FROM 
	OCRD 
WHERE
	OCRD.CardType = 'S'

regards LOthar

entry.title: Pop up when using Post goods receipt for Purchasing document
entry.content:
Hi All

We recently activated Scope Item 5HN which allows you to post Synchronous goods receipt and avoid the Inbound delivery. Now the App: Post Inbound delivery had one advantage compared to the other process. It would give you a Pop-up that displayed the text from the PO (Header Note) when you entered the PO.  

Now 

Now my Question is: Is possible to create this Pop-up for the App: Post goods Receipt for Purchasing Document? 

The App itself is very good but sadly it does not display any texts that are either in the Header of the PO oder the Item level texts. i think maybe it is possible to create a custom logic but my skills are not advanced enough for that. maybe i am missing something obvious.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment and ADT Troubleshooting Guide
entry.content: Very helpful blog, Helped me solve my issue

entry.title: Re: SAP ERP Central Component
entry.content:
Hi Waddah,

In the SAP ECC system, all the standard table field descriptions are available while you browse the table using the transaction code SE16, in the case of a custom table you may have to define.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: Transportation Management for own fleet
entry.content:
Hello Kiran

Yes, we only want to perform planning without incurring any charges. However, we are unable to achieve this in the standard Transportation Management.

Best Regardgs

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Business Catalog missing in App "Manage Launchpad Pages"
entry.content:
Hi @sebastian_wilhelm1,

does it appear under "Manually Selected"? Did you assign a launchpad app descriptor item to the IAM app in ADT?

Best regards
Kai

entry.title: Agent user required for installing diagnostic agent
entry.content:
When installing the diagnostic agent, do I need to connect to Solution Manager java and create an agent user?

Please confirm.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Agent user required for installing diagnostic agent
entry.content:
Dear customer,

For a newly installed Diagnostics Agent, you need to prepare a NetWeaver UME user for initial connection to SAP Solution Manager NetWeaver Java Application Server.

Please refer to instruction in step 1.2) of below KBA:

2385361 - How to connect Diagnostics Agent to SAP Solution Manager 7.2 system - SAP for Me

To use the Root Cause Analytics function of Solution Manager, it's required to connect the Diagnostics Agent to Solution Manager java. For a newly installed Diagnostics Agent, you need provide FQDN where SAP Solution Manager Java SCS instance is located, Java SCS Message Server HTTP Port and Credentials with User, Password. Please have a look at step 2) Diagnostic Agent Installation in above link.

Please do not hesitate to let us know if you have questions.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: We are looking for a Technology, Software, Gas, Oil, and Energy niche website.
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are currently seeking websites in the Technology, Software, Gas, Oil, and Energy sectors. If you own or manage a website in any of these niches, please send the details to ashish.amoli@bursys.com. Additionally, if you know any journalists or reporters working in these fields, kindly share their LinkedIn profiles and email addresses so we can connect with them directly.

Thank you!"

entry.title: Re: Ho to create a Testuser for RAP App in BTP?
entry.content:
Hi @sebastian_wilhelm1 ,

did you have a look at https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/identity-and-access-management-iam and https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/apps-identity-and-access-management?

Additionally, https://developers.sap.com/group.abap-env-authorizations.html might be helpful.

Best regards
Kai

entry.title: How we can assign the asset depreciation to the division wise please explain me the process.
entry.content:
i am giving here the example for better understanding 

Division is ice-cream for this we are maintaining the whole sales as the distribution channels for transporting of the that ice cream we are using  the one asset for doing depreciation run how we can assign this depreciation to the division. please  give me the process it requires urgent

Thanking you

entry.title: Re: How to mange and distribute fix in custom package to...
entry.content: Also this https://yelcho.github.io/sapui5-deployer/

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 - Summary
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 delivered an inspiring keynote with a strong focus on how technology drives real-world business innovations. This session brought to light the latest advances in AI, cloud solutions, and SAP's vision for the future of enterprise software.

The keynote opened with a thought-provoking introduction about the human brain, its complexities, and how innovation emerges from millions of connections forming new ideas. This metaphor seamlessly tied into SAP's philosophy of turning ideas into tangible solutions—where concepts become tools for solving real business problems.

Innovation at the Heart of SAP’s Strategy

One of the central themes was the growing importance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP’s offerings. AI is now deeply integrated into SAP’s cloud-driven portfolio, designed to simplify customers' lives and meet evolving business needs. Senior AI Scientists at SAP are working on harnessing AI to make business software smarter and more intuitive. The keynote also emphasized SAP's efforts to ensure that its software is configurable, extensible, and unified, providing a seamless suite-like experience across its platforms.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

A significant highlight was SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP), which has seen rapid growth in customer adoption. BTP is the cornerstone for SAP’s cloud and AI-driven future, empowering businesses with application development, automation, data analytics, and AI capabilities all within one unified platform. The keynote highlighted that over 27,000 live customers and 2,800 partners are leveraging SAP BTP to drive innovation.

The announcement of new BTP regions running on infrastructure from partners like AWS, Microsoft, and Google, along with SAP-managed data centers, shows SAP’s commitment to providing broader regional coverage and reliable service. This expansion aims to help businesses operate globally while maintaining efficiency, security, and compliance.

Enhancements in Application Development and Automation

SAP unveiled its Clean Core Strategy, which allows businesses to build custom extensions in a way that keeps the core ERP system maintainable and upgradeable. With SAP’s low-code environment, developers can create AI-powered applications with ease, while ABAP Cloud now supports both on-stack and side-by-side development, giving businesses more flexibility in their customizations.

One exciting feature introduced was the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, a tool designed to streamline the process of creating custom fields and extensions, allowing users to enhance SAP applications with minimal coding.

SAP Build and GenAI

In keeping with the AI theme, SAP showcased the GenAI capabilities within SAP Build. With Joule, an AI assistant, developers can create complex data models, build extensions, and generate applications through natural language prompts. This AI-driven approach reduces manual coding and helps users integrate advanced functionalities like time series forecasting and predictive analytics into their business processes.

Data and Analytics Innovations

The keynote further highlighted innovations in data and analytics, specifically focusing on SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Datasphere. The HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine were introduced, both of which enable businesses to harness structured and unstructured data for more context-aware AI-driven decisions. These engines promise faster and more accurate data processing, particularly in scenarios involving large language models and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

Additionally, the keynote showcased how SAP Datasphere is being adopted by large enterprises like Hershey’s and Bosch for data cataloging, integration, and self-service analytics, empowering businesses to access trusted data with ease.

Integration Suite and AI-Driven Optimization

The SAP Integration Suite now offers a modern, AI-powered solution for real-time end-to-end business process integration. With thousands of pre-built integrations and connectors, businesses can seamlessly integrate SAP and third-party applications. SAP is also leveraging AI-generated recommendations to optimize custom scripts, further enhancing developer productivity and reducing memory consumption.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 demonstrated SAP’s unwavering commitment to making innovation accessible, impactful, and transformative. From the integration of advanced AI capabilities to the expansion of BTP, SAP is setting a new standard in enterprise software. Whether you are looking to enhance your application development, automate processes, or harness the power of data, SAP’s latest innovations offer powerful tools to bring your ideas to life.

As SAP continues to evolve, the future of enterprise technology is more connected, intelligent, and adaptable than ever before.

entry.title: Re: Could not start Layout Designer (see long text)

entry.title: Industry Best Practices while implementing SAP Quality Management
FIORI: display expanded data just for one, selected record
entry.content:
Hi, I am trying to expand data in my fiori app. I am using example from


https://www.techippo.com/2017/09/bind-expand-sapui5-xml-view.html

As we can see, in example we have a full list of all flights:
items="{path : '/FlightSet' , parameters:{expand : 'ToFlightSchedules,ToFlightBooking'}

I need to get data about only one selected flight. I can not use something like this:
items="{path : '/FlightSet('id_of_something')' , parameters:{expand : 'ToFlightSchedules,ToFlightBooking'}
because I have error (to many apostrophes 😉 ).

Of course, I have prepared getaway on backend, which gives me correct data, for example:

/PracownicySet?$expand=toSzkolenia - it returns me expanded data for all records
/PracownicySet('00744272')?$expand=toSzkolenia,toHistoriaStanowisk - it returns me expanded data only for record with id 00744272.

What should I do to display expanded data just for one, selected record?

entry.title: Dear SAP Community i need a validation for restricted an warehouse for same SAP users

entry.title: Re: Pricing error in item Message no. VF073
entry.content:
Hi,

If the mandatory conditions are not met, please complete them.

entry.title: generate xml from cobol copybook
entry.content:
HI Guys,

i have requirement below to generate xml file based on cobol copybook definition

input file :

02305040624C 0B02305YPROCTER & GAMBLE/CLAIROL
02305040624C 1C040624 000013485210320
04295040624C 0B04295YTOPCO/PACIFIC COAST PRODUCERS
04295040624C 1C040624 000032280210300
14915040624C 0B14915YCAMPBELL SALES CO.
14915040624C 1C040624 000754380210300
02305041324C 0B02305YPROCTER & GAMBLE/CLAIROL
02305041324C 1C041324 000011500210320
02135040624D 0B02135YNESTLE USA
02135040624D 1C040624 001593510210300
02135040624D 324092000159351002135NESTLE USA D 

cobol copy book 

01 COUPONS-INPUT-RECORD.
05 CI-SYSTEM-KEY.
10 CI-SYS-VENDOR PIC X(05).
10 CI-SYS-PROMO PIC X(06).
10 CI-SYS-PAY-TYPE PIC X(01).
10 CI-SYS-FILLER PIC X(01).
05 CI-REC-TYPE PIC X(01).
88 CI-HEADER-REC VALUE '0'.
88 CI-DETAIL-REC VALUE '1' '2'.
88 CI-PAYMENT-REC VALUE '3'.
88 CI-COMMENT-REC VALUE '9'.
05 CI-HEADER-INFO.
10 CI-CLAIM-TYPE PIC X(01).
10 CI-VENDOR PIC X(05).
10 CI-RELEASE-FLAG PIC X(01).
10 CI-HEADER-VNDR-NAME PIC X(30).
10 CI-HEADER-FILLER PIC X(29).
05 CI-DETAIL-INFO REDEFINES CI-HEADER-INFO.
10 CI-COUPON-DESC.
15 CI-DESC-TYPE PIC X(01).
88 CI-DESC-PROMO VALUE 'C'.
88 CI-DESC-FEE VALUE 'F'.
15 CI-PROMO PIC X(06).
15 CI-OCR PIC X(06).
10 CI-AMOUNT PIC X(09).
10 CI-GL-ACCOUNT PIC X(06).
10 CI-DETAIL-FILLER PIC X(38).
05 CI-PAYMENT-INFO REDEFINES CI-HEADER-INFO.
10 CI-PAYMENT-DATE PIC X(06).
for Rest PD = Entry Date
10 CI-PAYMENT-AMT PIC X(09).
10 CI-PAYMENT-VENDOR PIC X(05).
10 CI-PAYMENT-VNDR-NAME PIC X(30).
10 CI-PAYMENT-TYPE PIC X(01).
88 CI-PAYMENT-CHECK VALUE 'C'.
88 CI-PAYMENT-INVDED VALUE 'D'.
88 CI-PAYMENT-CRMEMO VALUE 'M'.
10 CI-PAYMENT-FILLER PIC X(15).
05 CI-COMMENT-INFO REDEFINES CI-HEADER-INFO.
10 CI-COMMENT-NBR PIC 9(01).
10 CI-COMMENT.
15 CI-COMMENT-1ST-15 PIC X(15).
88 SPECIAL-ACCOUNT-LIT VALUE 'SPECIAL ACCOUNT'.
15 CI-COMMENT-REMAINDER PIC X(45).
10 CI-COMMENT-FILLER PIC X(05).

need to generate xml please help me how to deal with above files

entry.title: Re: Why confirmed date not displaying in CO09
entry.content: V_V2 is not supported with advanced ATP or gATP managed material. Hence, please adjust the Tag list to prevent confusion..

entry.title: The lrepconnector connect in index.html does not work
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I am fighting to load them the Flex Changes from LREP, configure the output routes in the XS-App.json, configure the manifest.json file with "flexenabled": True and integrating the library "sap.ui.fl "And in the index.html add this property data-sup-ui-flexibilityservices = '[{" connector ":" lrepconnector "}]' However when the deployment in SAP BTP I do not see that any call is made to"/sap/bc/lrep/flex/data/"that are configured on the routes of the XS -app.json, has it happened to them? I'm somewhat frustrated

perform a test at the terminal testing the destination and it works correctly when you try to obtain the data.

Regards

entry.title: How to get SAML Assertion info through Business Accelerator Hub by Application Configurations API
entry.content:
Try to use Application Configurations API under / SAP Cloud Identity Services to get SAML Assertion info. Navigation to https://api.sap.com/api/SCI_Application_Directory/resource/Applications. Found there is a Get API, in the ApplicationResponse, there is a node of Authentication Schema as below. Need that response info to get SAML Assertion for later use.

 

created an application with a lots of configurations like trust->Single Sign-on-> SAML2.0 Configuration, 

then use the application id to try out the Application Configurations API as below

However, in the response json body, do not found the Authentication-Schema. see below response body:

 

{
  "id": "e9d4c36e-97ab-4d8e-8651-d5bea4c2a593",
  "meta": {
    "created": "2024-10-08T06:43:20.857Z",
    "createdBy": "P000065",
    "lastModified": "2024-10-09T03:06:52.497Z",
    "modifiedBy": "P000065",
    "location": "https://rgmdevusconsummer.accounts.ondemand.com/Applications/v1/e9d4c36e-97ab-4d8e-8651-d5bea4c2a593",
    "type": "bundled",
    "version": "1"
  },
  "name": "https://ok4frtju9gfy25vfjgqhpal.authentication.us31.hana.ondemand.com",
  "multiTenantApp": false,
  "globalAccount": "e92e2c9c-ea09-4c43-a061-a136a7cfcca0",
  "schemas": [
    "urn:sap:identity:application:schemas:core:1.0"
  ],
  "branding": {
    "displayName": "anna-sac-test1",
    "showDisplayNameOnLogonScreen": true,
    "rememberMeVisible": true,
    "rememberMeChecked": false,
    "refreshParent": true,
    "tokenUrlEmbedCharacter": ";",
    "theme": {
      "type": "horizon"
    }
  }
}

 

Question: How can I use the Get API to get the SAML Assert info from the Authentication-Schema

entry.title: Re: Tax procedure
entry.content: Hi, Problem is solved by clearing the cache in edge. In Public Edition we have not much use for the transaction codes or function keys.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS02– What’s New
entry.content:
We have recently released the latest updates for SAP EWM with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 2 update. The enhancements we deliver not only further improve and compliment the SAP EWM features of the SAP S/4HANA 2023 release, but also expand integration options for EWM, such as integration with Service Order Management and extended Service Parts Planning, streamline processing steps for the Next Generation Just-In-Time (nJIT) external replenishment when using embedded S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management, Private Edition , enable circular logistic flows for returnable and reusable packaging material, offer new field extensibility options for select Warehouse Monitor nodes, and much more. The following blog post will provide an overview of the main highlights of this release, and we begin with an exploration of how this release further expands our best-of-suite integration capabilities.

INTEGRATION

Advanced Shipping & Receiving Enhancements

As with every update since the initial release of the Advanced Shipping & Receiving integration between SAP EWM and SAP TM, we invested in streamlining and expanding the capabilities of ASR. One such streamlining is the introduction of the Outbound “Streaming” process in the warehouse. What this means is that now warehouse workers can start to load available handling units (HUs) for an outbound delivery order, even if some items or HUs in the order have not been picked, packed or staged. Before the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 release, loading could only start when all HU’s in an outbound delivery order were ready for shipping. Now, with immediate loading, you can start the loading process for any handling unit that has the status “Packed for Shipping”. Immediate loading helps to reduce the total lead time needed for the outbound delivery. At the same time, it allows the staging areas to be used more efficiently by letting you load goods onto the truck instead of having them remain in the staging area, taking up valuable space.

Immediate loading of Handling UnitsOther Advanced Shipping & Receiving features we deliver in this release include:

  • The ability to schedule the automatic creation of waves from freight orders in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. This can be executed for both transportation planning type E (the delivery needs to be released in TM before warehouse processing can begin in EWM) and F (warehouse processing can begin immediately, and planning in TM can only be finalized when the outbound delivery order has the shipping readiness status set to “Ready”), and can be used for order-based as well as delivery-based freight order planning.
  • An enhancement to the RF loading and unloading screens which allow warehouse operators to use the container document type for radio frequency (RF) loading and unloading in Advanced Shipping and Receiving, depending on the deployment option. Please note that If you're using embedded Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) in SAP S/4HANA, you can use the container document type for RF loading and unloading. However, when using decentralized EWM based on SAP S/4HANA, you can use the container document type for RF loading only.

New RF logic transactions: “Loading by Container” for outbound process & “Unloading by Container” for inbound process

Service Order Management Integration

With this integration, users can now find an outbound delivery order (ODO) using the service order ID. When searching by service order, the system returns the outbound delivery along with references to the reservation and the service order. Users can also create a return delivery using the service order ID. Searching for orders using the service order ID improves visibility and control over service order fulfillment and returns processing.

Warehouse Management Monitor - Outbound Delivery node

The search criteria for the service order ID are available from the advanced search for the outbound delivery order and the inbound delivery. It is also integrated into the Warehouse Management Monitor. Via the Warehouse Monitor, users can display multiple ODO items for multiple Service Orders and trigger follow-up actions.

Other Integration Updates

To round off this category of updates, some more highlights that are worth mentioning are:

  • Transfer of Recommended Storage Quantity from Extended Service Parts Planning (eSPP) to Warehouse Product – with this release, users can now enable Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) to take over the requirement quantity, number of order items, or recommended storage quantity from Extended Service Parts Planning (eSPP) during slotting and update these values in the warehouse product master data (transaction /SCWM/MAT1). For the technical details on how to set up the eSPP data access for the EWM slotting customizing, please read SAP Note 836899 “TDL: Setup of customizing for EWM slotting->SPP data access”
  • Quality Certificates in Goods Receipt with Synchronous Goods Posting – we have further expanded on our Quality Management integration to allow users to process quality certificates for synchronous goods receipt posting in the storage locations managed by Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA. Users can also confirm the receipt of a quality certificate when posting a goods receipt for a purchase order in the Post Goods Movement app (transaction MIGO). The quality certificate not only certifies the quality of the goods, it also records the chemical and physical properties, the inspection results, and the batch characteristics.

Activating Quality certificate process in EWM customizing "Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs"

  • Quality Inspections by Generic Article in Retail (Decentralized EWM) – one final quality management topic to mention is that now users can perform quality inspections for generic articles instead of article variants when using decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) based on SAP S/4HANA. For example, a user can inspect generic article Shirt 0815 instead of article variant Shirt 0815, Color Red, Size M. These quality planning properties can be specified in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Quality Management > Inspection Creation > BAdI: Data Transfer to Inspection Properties.

PRODUCTION INTEGRATION

Assign Multiple Bins to a Production Supply Area (PSA) for Repetitive Manufacturing (REM)

In the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 release, SAP introduced the ability to assign multiple bins for each Production Supply Area for MES-Driven staging for production & process orders, with the option to do follow-up actions, such as consumption triggered from a Manufacturing Execution System (MES), clear of Production Supply Area, etc. Now with the latest release, users can manage a production supply area (PSA) more granularly by dividing it into multiple storage bins in MES-Driven Repetitive Manufacturing. This lets you store stock separately and supply it to different resources or different production process steps in a work centre. It is now easier to keep track of the stock and ensure that the right products are available for each production process in MES-driven Repetitive Manufacturing.

Assigning multiple bins process overview

 Just as with production and process orders, users can use multiple bins assigned to PSAs for a component when working with confirmations send by a manufacturing execution system (MES) in the integration of repetitive manufacturing with Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

To implement this, warehouse clerks need to assign the storage bins to the product, party entitled to dispose, and PSA, in the Assign Bin to PSA app (transaction /SCWM/PSASTAGE2) and select MES-Relevant and Allow Multiple Bin Assignment. Then, they must define multiple storage bins for the product, party entitled to dispose, and PSA in the Assign Multiple Bins to PSA app (transaction /SCWM/MBINPSA).

Assigning multiple bins implementation example

JIT Call-Triggered Supply to Production (S2P) or to Customer (S2C) for Component Group Materials

But aside from the ability to assign multiple bins to a Production Supply Area for repetitive manufacturing, the largest block of production related improvements is the ability to fulfill a supply-to-production (S2P) call by receiving the required component group materials from a supplier to production. In some industries, vendors deliver grouped materials / components, which when received by the warehouse, are moved directly to the PSA for production. S2P stock is a new special stock category, where the S2P number would be the JIT call number which triggers the supply to production event. The S2P stock could also be posted to Supply to Customer (S2C) stock.

Here's how the S2P inbound process works:

  1. An S2P Just-In-Time (JIT) call is triggered to create an inbound delivery for component group materials in Delivery Management (LE-SHP).
  2. Transportation planning is completed in Transportation Management. Once the Ready for Warehouse Processing status is set for the relevant freight order, the delivery of Delivery Management is updated to include items for component materials.
  3. An inbound delivery in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), containing only component group materials, is now active for processing.
  4. Goods receipt is posted for the inbound delivery in EWM, and a goods receipt message is sent to Delivery Management.
  5. Finally, the put away process is done for the handling unit containing the S2P stock in the production supply area specified by the JIT call.

A Bill of Material example with Components and Component Group handling units

 To support this, we have also developed a Combined Goods Movement Service (API). This application programming interface (API) enables users to post goods movements (goods receipts, goods issues, transfer postings, and their reversals) in Inventory Management and Extended Warehouse Management. The API allows combinations of different goods movements to be posted in the same API call.

Process flow example for Combined Goods Movement Service

This API supports the following Goods Movement:

  • Combined Goods Movements
  • Goods Movements in IM only using IM managed storage locations
  • Goods Movements in EWM only for S2P/S2C stock
  • GR of HUs in EWM for normal stock and S2P/S2C stock
  • Support of auxiliary packaging materials
  • Automatic putaway warehouse task creation
  • GR of Serial numbers for normal materials/stock
  • GR of batches for normal materials/stock
  • GI from HUs for normal stock and S2P/S2C stock

Tying up this topic,, we also enable users to perform the warehouse processing steps for the Next Generation Just-In-Time (nJIT) external replenishment processes for multiple Production Supply Areas when using Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA. This includes the following activities with inbound delivery items for multiple production supply areas (PSAs):

  • Receipt of nJIT inbound deliveries for items containing handling units (HUs) with different destination PSAs
  • Status update of nJIT calls when confirming putaway warehouse tasks to the PSAs

CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENTS

Enhancements to Goods Distribution Process Using Distribution Equipment

Initially released in 2019 for SAP EWM, the introduction of distribution equipment supported warehouse operators in transporting goods in a warehouse to different sections in the facility, for example, components to production supply areas (PSAs). With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 release, we further expand the scenarios and capabilities in which distribution equipment can be used in logistics facilities:

  • Load handling units (HUs) during the unloading process - Using a radio frequency (RF) presentation device, users can load handling units (HUs) onto distribution equipment after they have already started the unloading process. When loading during unloading, a warehouse operator must specify the HU position on the distribution equipment manually. After the operator starts unloading and scans an HU to identify the distribution equipment, they can load HUs instead of confirming the next unloading warehouse task. For example, they can collect empty HUs from a production supply area (PSA) while unloading HUs and bring them to an empties buffer zone. Furthermore, users can also collect HUs containing semi-finished components from a PSA and bring them to the next PSA for further production steps.

/SCWM/RFUI Navigation: 04 Outbound Process > 06 Distribution Equip. > 04 Unloading by HU

  • Use distribution equipment in the external replenishment process for Just-In-Time (JIT) or Just-In-Sequence (JIS) calls – The integration of Next Generation Just-In-Time (JIT), Delivery Management (LE-SHP), Transportation Management (TM), and Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) enables the receival of component materials based on JIT calls and to store them in the production supply area. With the latest release, warehouse workers can now use distribution equipment to move goods in the external replenishment process to PSAs. The following RF screens have been added for external replenishment for JIT or JIS calls:
  • 03 Inbound Processes > 06 Distribution Equipment > 01 Load by HU - Auto. Pos. Assignment
  • 03 Inbound Processes > 06 Distribution Equipment > 02 Load by HU - Man. Pos. Assignment
  • 03 Inbound Processes > 06 Distribution Equipment > 03 Change Loading/Unloading Details
  • 03 Inbound Processes > 06 Distribution Equipment > 04 Unload by HU

Ad Hoc Movement of HUs – Lastly, with this release we extended the API for goods distribution with distribution equipment to enable users to create ad hoc warehouse tasks for HUs to use distribution equipment for the supply of components to production. Warehouse operators can also use the monitor method Change HU Destination Data to change or delete the destination PSA and destination storage bin of one or more lowest-level HUs.

Maintain HU Destination Data via Monitor Method

Reverse Goods Receipt for Handling Units from Production

With this feature, we enable users to reverse the goods receipt of a handling unit (HU) received from production with a corresponding Logistics Execution (LE) delivery after final putaway. Since SAP S/4HANA 2023, we made it possible to reverse goods receipt of HUs after final putaway for HUs received using Advanced Production Integration without a corresponding LE delivery, where the goods receipt is communicated using the goods movement interface. Now users can reverse the goods receipt for all HUs received from production, including HUs with a corresponding LE delivery. The goods receipt doesn’t have to be communicated using the goods movement interface. If a decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) based on SAP S/4HANA system is used, the connected enterprise management system must allow the reversal of completed LE deliveries.

Reverse Goods Receipt from Product process flow

 OTHER RELEASE HIGHLIGHTS

In this final section, I would like to round off today’s blog post by mentioning a few more enhancements that we have delivered that will improve and streamline the user experiences:

  • Migration of LE-WM Data Using an XML File – To simplify data migration of LE-WM data, we have enabled users to download warehouse storage bins and warehouse stock from their legacy LE-WM system to an XML file. The XML file can then be used to upload the data to the Extended Warehouse Management system using the migration cockpit. To download the warehouse storage bins or warehouse stock, on the SAP Easy Access menu users must choose Extended Warehouse Management > Interfaces > Migration from LE-WM > LE-WM Migration via Migration Cockpit or call transaction /SCWM/MIG_FILE_MC in their Extended Warehouse Management system, instead of /SCWM/MIG_BIN and /SCWM/MIG_STOCK.

Migration of LE-WM data using an XML File process flow

  • Serialized Returnable Packaging Materials – Prior to this release, serial numbers were assigned to the contents of an HU, but not to the HU itself. As a result, the system could not automatically generate a packaging item correspondent to the HU in the outbound delivery order. However now with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, users can assign a serial number (SN) to a returnable packaging material that is used to pack a handling unit (HU) by entering the serial number in the HU header. This unique serial number helps to ensure that the correct returnable packaging material can be included in the goods issue. The serial number also allows the system to automatically generate a packaging item that corresponds to the HU.

If activated, a SN field will appear when creating an HU for a packaging material that has a SN profile assigned in the material master

  • Custom Fields for Selection Screens in the Warehouse Management Monitor – Lastly, want I want to mention is that now users can add custom fields to some of the selection screens in the warehouse management monitor. These new fields can then be used when selecting, for example, warehouse orders, warehouse tasks, or deliveries relevant for inbound, outbound, or other processes. If these Business Add-In (BAdI) methods to support variant data handling are implemented, users can also store selection values for the custom fields in monitor node selection variants.

Warehouse Monitor with Selection Screen for Outbound Delivery Order Items

To add custom selection field, users must go in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Extended Warehouse Management > Monitoring > Warehouse Management Monitor > BAdI: Custom Selection Fields in Standard Monitor Nodes.

Custom fields can be added for the following monitor nodes and subnodes:

  • Inbound > Documents > Inbound Delivery and subnodes
  • Outbound > Documents > Outbound Delivery Order and subnodes
  • Outbound > Documents > Production Material Request and subnodes
  • Documents > Posting Changes and Posting Change Items subnode
  • Documents > Stock Transfer and Stock Transfer Items subnode
  • Stock and Bin > Storage Bin > Handling Unit
  • Stock and Bin > Handling Unit
  • Documents > Handling Unit

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above mentioned topics (such as examples of configuration screens), as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release, I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP EWM in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 – What’s New” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: Re: I am unable to log in with SAP* or DDIC user passwords
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: Validate FI Document
entry.content: FI Validation GGB0, create a validation at item level.

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @meenakshi_si ,

It is not necessary to manually upload 'display name' for new Employee every day. You can leave it empty, then system will generate 'display name' automatically based on settings of COMMON_User_Display_Name in text replacement. 

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Re: 手动创建计划订单，当某个组件在MRP4设置了后继料时，计划订单的组件清单中没有带出来后继物料。只有手动创建...
entry.content:
如果用户通过 MD11 系统手动创建计划订单，系统将分配停产组件而不是后续物料。您可能需要考虑可扩展性以适应变化。建议您对相关产品执行 MRP 运行。一旦您计划停产物料，MRP 将重定向采购提案中可用的后续物料和数量。

对于给定的示例，WSG022 是停产物料，RSG022 是后续物料。

FERT 物料的 PIR 为 100 件，并且已执行 MRP。已创建 100 件的计划订单。

仓库中有可供停产和后续商品使用的库存。

在MRP运行期间，系统检查物料主数据中的停产指示符和有效期，并相应地重新分配物料。系统在计划运行期间重新分配停产物料的需求。

下面的屏幕截图显示了 FERT 计划订单组件概览。在这里我们可以看到，停产物料 WSG022 的可用库存数量为 10，而后续物料 RSG022 的剩余数量为 90。

附加参考：

SAP Library - BOMs (PP-BD-BOM)

Concept of Discontinuation Data - Material Master ... - SAP Community

Solved: MD11 generates qty for discontinued component - SAP Community

entry.title: 2H2024 access self-assigned learning activities that have been removed from users library.
entry.content:
Hello,

which option (e.g. report) can be used to check whether there are learning activities on the learning plans that are no longer in the library.
For cases in which the courses are no longer to be completed.

Best Regards

Conny

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Extensibility: All You Need to Know
entry.content:
Hi all,

here is another interesting document I want to share: the new paper on Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, now available at SAP.com and in the PDF Digital Library. 

Best regards, Thomas

entry.title: Re: Join BSEG, BKPF, EKBE and EKPO
entry.content: yes i got a result, but it did not show me any views with more than one table. i only got e.g. a view where table EKPO was used, then another view where the table EKBE was used, etc.

entry.title: I have error message IDMX200 with F-92
entry.content:
Error while trying to use F-92  in MEXICO for an Asset

Can you please help? message " Unexpected Customizing error" IDMX200

Thanks

Alfredo
